# UC-22.4.18 · CCPA/CPRA — Minor Consent — Marketing Cookie Fires Before Parental Consent Timestamp

## Description

Tracking technologies on minor-directed experiences require heightened controls. Tag-manager events that load ad or analytics pixels prior to recorded parental consent timestamps evidence misconfiguration of consent mode integrations.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for CCPA/CPRA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes web and sourcetypes consent:web, tag:fire. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Scope to minor-designated site IDs via lookup `minor_site_ids.csv`; (2) partner with marketing ops on tag inventory; (3) GPC and CMP linkage testing in pre-prod; (4) document vendor contracts; (5) evidence for AG inquiries on dark pattern adjacent issues. Indexes required: `index=web`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=consent:web`, `sourcetype=tag:fire`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Tealium/Segment/GTM server-side logs via HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=web` `sourcetype="consent:web"` (session_id, parental_consent_ts, consent_string); `index=web` `sourcetype="tag:fire"` (session_id, tag_vendor, marketing_flag, _time).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Scope to minor-designated site IDs via lookup `minor_site_ids.csv`; (2) partner with marketing ops on tag inventory; (3) GPC and CMP linkage testing in pre-prod; (4) document vendor contracts; (5) evidence for AG inquiries on dark pattern adjacent issues.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=web sourcetype="consent:web" earliest=-30d
| eval pc=strptime(parental_consent_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="tag:fire" marketing_flag=true earliest=-30d
    | fields session_id, tag_vendor, _time
  ]
| where _time < pc OR isnull(pc)
| stats count by tag_vendor, session_id
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CCPA/CPRA — Minor Consent — Marketing Cookie Fires Before Parental Consent Timestamp** — Tracking technologies on minor-directed experiences require heightened controls. Tag-manager events that load ad or analytics pixels prior to recorded parental consent timestamps evidence misconfiguration of consent mode integrations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=web` `sourcetype="consent:web"` (session_id, parental_consent_ts, consent_string); `index=web` `sourcetype="tag:fire"` (session_id, tag_vendor, marketing_flag, _time). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Tealium/Segment/GTM server-side logs via HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: web; **sourcetype**: consent:web. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=web, sourcetype="consent:web", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pc** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time < pc OR isnull(pc)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **tag_vendor, session_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: pc, session_id, tag_vendor.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sessions), Bar by vendor, Single value (violations).

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype="consent:web" earliest=-30d
| eval pc=strptime(parental_consent_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="tag:fire" marketing_flag=true earliest=-30d
    | fields session_id, tag_vendor, _time
  ]
| where _time < pc OR isnull(pc)
| stats count by tag_vendor, session_id
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (sessions), Bar by vendor, Single value (violations). Key fields: `pc`, `session_id`, `tag_vendor`.
